# SkillFade

> SkillFade is a skill-decay tracker for developers and self-directed learners.
> It measures whether your skills are staying fresh or quietly fading — the
> opposite of a streak counter. A calm mirror, not a coach.

## What it does
- Tracks a 0–100% freshness score per skill using exponential decay anchored to your last practice.
- Separates learning events (input: reading, videos, courses) from practice events (output: projects, exercises, work).
- Surfaces three signals: learning decay, practice scarcity, and input/output imbalance.
- No gamification: no streaks, badges, points, or leaderboards.

## Key concepts
- Skill decay / learning decay: the gradual loss of a skill over time without reinforcement (the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve).
- Freshness score: a 0–100% estimate of how sharp a skill currently is.
- Input/output balance: the ratio of practice (producing) to learning (consuming); above 1.0 is where retention lives.

## Pricing
- Free core, plus an optional one-time SkillFade PRO upgrade (lifetime access, no subscription).

- Home: https://skillfade.website/
- What is learning decay: https://skillfade.website/what-is-learning-decay
- The skill decay formula: https://skillfade.website/skill-decay-formula
- Learning vs practice: https://skillfade.website/learning-vs-practice
- Features: https://skillfade.website/features
- Pricing: https://skillfade.website/pricing
- Comparisons (vs Anki, Notion, Obsidian): https://skillfade.website/comparisons
- Blog: https://skillfade.website/blog
